mnemonics: D.O.G.S. (Dynamic Obstacle Guidance System) - Dynamic Obstacle avoidance, Gradient-based planning, Sensor fusion
keyPoints: Reactive methods like Potential Fields create repulsive forces around obstacles.
Sampling-based planners (e.g., RRT) build a feasible path by exploring the space.
Optimization-based methods penalize paths that intersect with obstacles in the cost function.
commonMistakes: Getting stuck in local minima (e.g., a U-shaped obstacle).
Underestimating computational complexity for real-time navigation.
Failing to account for the robot's physical dimensions and dynamics.
